results.description: STEP-01: 1 to 10 prioritized features, each with a WSJF score (number)
STEP-01: Lean Business Case present for the major features

output.visionBoard:
PI VISION BOARD — Digital Banking ART · Customer Portal Redesign (next PI)

TARGET CUSTOMERS / PERSONAS
- "Self-reliant Sophie" (digital-first retail customer): wants to manage everyday banking without calling the branch.
- "Cautious Claude" (low-confidence digital user): needs trust, security and clear guidance.
- "On-the-go Omar" (mobile-only): expects full feature parity on mobile.

PROBLEM / NEED
Legacy portal drives high call-center volume, low NPS, weak self-service for payments and complaints, and PSD2/SCA compliance gaps. Web and mobile experiences are inconsistent.

VISION STATEMENT
For retail customers who want to bank independently, the redesigned customer portal is a unified web + mobile self-care experience that lets them manage accounts, make payments, and resolve complaints securely in minutes — reducing reliance on branches and call centers, unlike the current fragmented portal.

TOP FEATURES THIS PI (priority order by WSJF)
1. SCA / PSD2 strong customer authentication
2. Accessibility & WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
3. Self-care account management (self-service)
4. Digital complaints management
5. Payments self-service (transfers + bill pay)
6. Real-time notifications & alerts
7. Mobile feature parity
8. Personalized customer dashboard

BUSINESS GOALS / TARGET OUTCOMES
- Reduce inbound call-center contacts on covered journeys by 25%.
- Increase digital self-service rate for payments & complaints to 70%.
- Raise portal NPS by +10 points; achieve 100% SCA/PSD2 compliance before regulatory deadline.
- Web/mobile parity ≥ 90% of in-scope journeys.

NON-NEGOTIABLES
- PSD2/SCA regulatory compliance, WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility, security & data protection (GDPR), audited complaint traceability.

ARCHITECTURAL / ENABLER FOCUS
- Shared design system (web + mobile), API gateway for SCA, event backbone for notifications.

KPIs / LEADING INDICATORS
- Self-service completion rate, call deflection %, SCA success rate, complaint resolution SLA, accessibility audit pass rate, mobile/web parity coverage.

DEPENDENCIES & RISKS
- Core banking & payment rails APIs, identity provider for SCA, regulatory deadline (time-critical), web/mobile team coordination.

TIMELINE: Single PI (5 iterations + IP). MVP = SCA + self-care account view + complaints intake by mid-PI; payments & mobile parity by end of PI.
output.leanBusinessCase:
LEAN BUSINESS CASE — Customer Portal Redesign (Digital Banking ART)

1. PROBLEM / OPPORTUNITY
The current portal generates high call-center cost, low NPS, fragmented web/mobile journeys, and exposes the bank to PSD2/SCA non-compliance penalties. Customers cannot self-serve key journeys (payments, complaints), driving expensive assisted channels.

2. SOLUTION HYPOTHESIS (MVP)
We believe that delivering a unified, secure self-care portal (SCA-compliant authentication + self-service account management + digital complaints + payments) will reduce call-center contacts and increase digital self-service. We will know we are right when call deflection reaches 25% and self-service rate on covered journeys reaches 70% within two PIs of release.

3. MAJOR FEATURES & RATIONALE
- SCA/PSD2 (WSJF 5.8): regulatory must-have, high time-criticality (deadline-driven), unlocks all payment journeys.
- Accessibility/WCAG (WSJF 7.0): low job size, high compliance + opportunity-enablement; legal exposure reduction.
- Self-care account management (3.6) & Complaints (3.6): core call-deflection drivers.
- Payments self-service (2.88): highest business value, larger job size.
- Notifications (3.67), Mobile parity (2.0), Dashboard (2.0): engagement and retention.

4. COST OF DELAY DRIVERS
Regulatory deadline (SCA), continued call-center cost (~per-contact savings), customer attrition to neobanks, brand/NPS erosion.

5. INVESTMENT (T-shirt)
~1 PI of Web + Mobile teams (8–10 person ART capacity) plus shared design-system and SCA enabler work. Largely opex; reuse of existing core-banking/payment APIs limits build cost.

6. EXPECTED BENEFITS
- Cost: 25% reduction in covered call-center volume.
- Revenue/retention: improved NPS (+10), reduced churn.
- Compliance: avoided PSD2 penalties, WCAG legal risk mitigated.

7. KEY ASSUMPTIONS & RISKS
Core/payment API readiness, identity provider availability for SCA, regulatory deadline holds, web/mobile coordination, change-adoption by customers.

8. GO / NO-GO RECOMMENDATION
GO — proceed with SCA + accessibility + self-care MVP first (highest WSJF, regulatory), validate deflection metrics, then incrementally fund payments, mobile parity and dashboard based on measured outcomes (pivot-or-persevere at PI boundary).

results.description: STEP-02: Program Board with dependencies non-empty

programBoard.dep: SCA enabler requires IdP integration for strong customer authentication; IdP availability gates start of MVP.
SCA flows routed through the API gateway; enabler must be ready before SCA feature work.
Transfers + bill pay depend on payment rails API readiness and reused core-banking APIs.
All payment journeys are gated by SCA; payments cannot ship before SCA is live.
Account overview & actions consume core-banking account/transaction APIs.
WCAG-compliant components must be delivered in the shared design system first.
Web journeys consume shared design-system components (cross-team).
Parity requires corresponding web journeys delivered first; web/mobile coordination via Scrum of Scrums.
Mobile consumes shared design-system tokens/components for parity.
Notifications depend on the event backbone being operational.

programBoard.to: Audited complaint traceability / GDPR data store
programBoard.dep: Complaint intake/tracking requires compliant, audited storage for SLA traceability.
Dashboard aggregates account data and notification feeds; depends on both features.

roamRisks.risk: Regulatory SCA/PSD2 deadline (time-critical) may not be met
roamRisks.action: Sequence SCA + accessibility as highest-WSJF MVP by mid-PI; track via ART Sync and System Demo.

roamRisks.risk: Core banking & payment rails API readiness
roamRisks.action: Confirm API contracts and readiness dates in IP iteration; weekly Scrum of Scrums follow-up.
Lock IdP integration window early; stub/mock for parallel development.

roamRisks.risk: Web/Mobile team coordination causing parity slippage
roamRisks.action: Shared design system + cross-team Scrum of Scrums; mobile follows web journey cadence.
Validate deflection & self-service KPIs post-release; pivot-or-persevere at PI boundary.

results.description: STEP-03: sprint 1 backlog of 5 to 10 User Stories
STEP-03: each backlog US carries a WSJF score (number)

piObjectives: Deliver the SCA/PSD2 strong customer authentication MVP — integrated with the external Identity Provider and routed through the API Gateway enabler — live in production for 100% of regulated payment journeys by mid-PI (Iteration 3, end of July 2026), meeting the PSD2 regulatory deadline (Business Value: 10).

Launch payments self-service (transfers + bill pay) on Web and Mobile, gated by live SCA and built on Core Banking & payment-rails APIs, reaching ≥ 60% self-service transaction adoption and ≥ 25% call-center deflection by end of PI (Business Value: 9).

Achieve WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance for 100% of shared Design System components consumed by Web and Mobile journeys, validated by automated + manual audit before the final System Demo (Business Value: 8).

Deliver Web/Mobile feature parity for self-care account management (account overview, transactions, profile actions) on Core Banking APIs, with ≤ 1 iteration lag between Web and Mobile releases, coordinated via Scrum of Scrums (Business Value: 7).

Stand up the event-backbone enabler to power real-time notifications and the personalized customer dashboard, delivering ≥ 95% in-app alert delivery within 5 seconds for account and payment events by end of PI (Stretch — Business Value: 6).

backlogSprint.statement: As the Platform/Security team, I want to integrate the external Identity Provider (IdP) for strong customer authentication, so that the SCA enabler can authenticate users and unblock the MVP. [Enabler — gates SCA, owned ROAM risk: IdP availability]
As the Platform team, I want the API Gateway enabler to route and secure SCA authentication flows, so that SCA feature work can begin against a ready gateway. [Enabler — dependency for SCA]

As the Platform/Design team, I want to deliver the first set of WCAG 2.1 AA-compliant components in the shared Design System, so that Web and Mobile teams can build accessible journeys. [Enabler — gates accessibility & all UI journeys]
As a customer, I want to complete an SCA strong-authentication challenge (MVP flow) when logging in, so that my access meets PSD2 requirements. [Time-critical regulatory]

As the RTE/Platform liaison, I want to confirm and stub Core Banking & payment-rails API contracts during the IP iteration, so that payments and self-care development can proceed in parallel. [Spike/Enabler — owned ROAM risk]

As the Platform team, I want to stand up the event-backbone enabler, so that real-time notifications and the dashboard can subscribe to account/payment events. [Enabler — dependency for notifications]

As a customer, I want a read-only self-care account overview (balance + recent transactions) on Web consuming Core Banking APIs, so that I can self-serve my account information. [Web feature on shared Design System]

As the Mobile team, I want to consume the shared Design System tokens/components for the account overview, so that Web/Mobile parity is maintained from the start. [Parity — coordinated via Scrum of Scrums]

As the Compliance/Platform team, I want to provision the audited, GDPR-compliant data store for complaint traceability, so that digital complaints intake can meet SLA and audit requirements. [Enabler — dependency for complaints]

results.description: STEP-04: sprint plan with forecast — `usEngagees` non-empty AND `storyPoints` (number)

output.sprintGoal: Establish the secure authentication foundation for the PSD2/SCA MVP by integrating the external Identity Provider and standing up the API Gateway enabler, while unblocking parallel UI and payments work through the first WCAG 2.1 AA Design System components and confirmed Core Banking & payment-rails API contracts.

usEngagees: [Enabler] Integrate the external Identity Provider (IdP) for strong customer authentication so the SCA enabler can authenticate users (WSJF 8.6) — ROAM risk: IdP availability owned
[Enabler] Stand up the API Gateway enabler to route and secure SCA authentication flows so SCA feature work can begin (WSJF 8.0)
[Enabler] Deliver the first set of WCAG 2.1 AA-compliant shared Design System components so Web and Mobile can build accessible journeys (WSJF 6.7)
[Spike/Enabler] Confirm and stub Core Banking & payment-rails API contracts during the IP iteration so payments and self-care can proceed in parallel (WSJF 5.0) — ROAM risk owned

impediments: IdP availability/readiness (ROAM-owned risk): external vendor dependency — sandbox/credentials and SLA must be secured early or IdP integration slips, cascading to the Iteration 3 regulatory SCA deadline.
API Gateway non-prod environment provisioning and security configuration may not be ready, blocking the routing of SCA flows.
Core Banking & payment-rails API contracts not yet finalized: payments and self-care teams remain blocked until stubs/contracts are confirmed during the IP iteration.
Accessibility tooling gap: automated + manual WCAG 2.1 AA audit pipeline not yet stood up for the Design System, risking late defects.
Dependency sequencing across teams: SCA feature teams stay idle until IdP + Gateway enablers complete — requires Scrum of Scrums coordination to smooth the handoff and avoid downstream idle time.
Concentration of value in enablers handled by the Platform/Security team creates a capacity bottleneck; cross-team load balancing should be reviewed at Scrum of Scrums.

results.description: STEP-06: PI dashboard carrying at least `avancement` AND `risques`
STEP-06: sprint EVM with CPI and SPI (numbers)

dashboard.avancement: ≈ 20% du PI (Itération 1 / foundation — jalon SCA réglementaire fixé à l'Itération 3)

risques.libelle: Disponibilité/readiness de l'IdP — sandbox, credentials et SLA fournisseur à sécuriser tôt.
risques.consequence: Glissement de l'intégration SCA → menace le jalon réglementaire Itération 3.
risques.mitigation: Escalade achat/vendor, contractualisation SLA, plan B fournisseur, suivi quotidien.

risques.libelle: Provisioning et configuration sécurité de l'API Gateway hors-prod non prêts.
risques.consequence: Blocage du routage des flux d'authentification SCA.
risques.mitigation: Prioriser le ticket infra, revue sécurité anticipée, environnement de repli.

risques.libelle: Contrats Core Banking & payment-rails non finalisés.
risques.consequence: Équipes paiements & self-care bloquées jusqu'aux stubs/contrats (IP iteration).
risques.mitigation: Confirmer & stubber les contrats pendant l'IP iteration, mocks contractuels.

risques.libelle: Pipeline d'audit WCAG 2.1 AA (auto + manuel) non encore opérationnel.
risques.consequence: Risque de défauts d'accessibilité tardifs sur le Design System.
risques.mitigation: Stand-up outillage axe-core + audit manuel dès les premiers composants.

risques.libelle: Équipes SCA en attente des enablers IdP+Gateway; valeur concentrée sur Platform/Security (goulot).
risques.consequence: Temps mort aval, surcharge équipe socle.
risques.mitigation: Coordination Scrum of Scrums, lissage de charge inter-équipes, travail anticipé non bloquant.

output.noteCodir:
NOTE CODIR — MVP PSD2/SCA — Itération 1 (Foundation) — 20/06/2026

1) MESSAGE CLÉ (RAG: AMBRE)
Le sprint pose le socle d'authentification sécurisée du MVP PSD2/SCA. Engagement de 34 points (4 items à forte valeur WSJF). Avancement PI ≈ 20%. Le statut est AMBRE en raison d'une dépendance externe critique (IdP) sur le chemin du jalon réglementaire de l'Itération 3.

2) OBJECTIF DE SPRINT
Établir le socle d'authentification forte (SCA) en intégrant l'Identity Provider externe et en montant l'enabler API Gateway, tout en débloquant en parallèle les travaux UI et Paiements via les premiers composants Design System WCAG 2.1 AA et la confirmation des contrats API Core Banking & payment-rails.

3) ENGAGEMENTS (34 SP)
- [Enabler] Intégration de l'IdP externe pour la SCA — WSJF 8.6 (risque IdP ROAM: Owned).
- [Enabler] Montée de l'API Gateway pour router/sécuriser les flux d'authentification SCA — WSJF 8.0.
- [Enabler] Premiers composants partagés Design System WCAG 2.1 AA pour Web & Mobile — WSJF 6.7.
- [Spike/Enabler] Confirmation et stubbing des contrats API Core Banking & payment-rails (IP iteration) — WSJF 5.0 (risque ROAM: Owned).

4) AVANCEMENT & EVM
Avancement PI ≈ 20% (Itération 1/foundation). CPI = 1,0 (coûts maîtrisés, conforme au budget). SPI = 0,95 (léger retard de calendrier piloté par la dépendance IdP). Hypothèse: baseline de début d'IP, valeurs à réactualiser dès les premiers actuals d'itération. Pas de dérive coût; surveillance rapprochée du délai.

5) RISQUES & POINTS DE BLOCAGE (RAID/ROAM)
- CRITIQUE: Disponibilité IdP (vendor) — peut faire glisser la SCA et menacer le jalon réglementaire Itération 3. Action: sécuriser sandbox/credentials/SLA en urgence.
- ÉLEVÉ: Provisioning & sécurité API Gateway hors-prod non prêts — bloque le routage SCA.
- ÉLEVÉ: Contrats Core Banking & payment-rails non finalisés — paiements/self-care bloqués jusqu'aux stubs.
- MOYEN: Pipeline d'audit accessibilité WCAG 2.1 AA non opérationnel — risque de défauts tardifs.
- MOYEN: Séquencement inter-équipes & goulot de capacité Platform/Security — temps mort aval des équipes SCA.

6) DÉCISIONS / ARBITRAGES DEMANDÉS AU CODIR
- Valider l'escalade fournisseur IdP (achats + contractualisation SLA) comme priorité n°1.
- Sécuriser la capacité infra pour le provisioning Gateway hors-prod.
- Arbitrer un éventuel renfort/lissage de charge sur l'équipe Platform/Security.

7) PROCHAINES ÉTAPES
- Scrum of Scrums dédié au séquencement IdP → Gateway → équipes SCA.
- Stand-up de l'outillage d'audit WCAG 2.1 AA dès les premiers composants.
- Confirmation des stubs de contrats API durant l'IP iteration pour débloquer les flux parallèles.
- Réactualisation EVM (CPI/SPI) au prochain point d'itération.
